title: Screen component — root container
content:
Always wrap the entire app in `<Screen>`. It clamps to `max-w-[430px]`, centers on desktop, and sets the dark/light background.

```tsx
import { Screen } from "./components/ui";

<Screen>
  <AppBar title="My App" />
  <main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-5 pb-32 space-y-4">
    {/* content */}
  </main>
</Screen>
```

The `<main>` element inside Screen should have `px-5` for edge padding and `pb-32` to clear FAB/TabBar.

title: AppBar — iOS-style top navigation
content:
Large-title iOS-style app bar that sits at the top inside `<Screen>`.

```tsx
import { AppBar, IconButton, MoonIcon, SunIcon } from "./components/ui";

<AppBar
  title="Tasks"
  subtitle="3 remaining"
  trailing={<IconButton onClick={toggleTheme}><MoonIcon/></IconButton>}
/>
```

Props: `title` (string), `subtitle` (string, optional), `trailing` (ReactNode, optional — use for dark mode toggle or settings icon).

title: TabBar — bottom navigation with 3-5 tabs
Button variants and sizes
content:
Use the pre-built `<Button>` for ALL buttons. Never create custom `<button>` elements with Tailwind classes.

```tsx
import { Button, PlusIcon } from "./components/ui";

<Button>Save</Button>                           // primary (default)
<Button variant="secondary">Cancel</Button>
<Button variant="ghost" size="sm">Skip</Button>
<Button variant="danger" fullWidth>Delete</Button>
<Button leading={<PlusIcon/>}>Add Item</Button>
```

Props: `variant` ("primary"|"secondary"|"ghost"|"danger"), `size` ("sm"|"md"|"lg"), `fullWidth` (boolean), `leading` (ReactNode for left icon).

title: IconButton — round icon-only button
content:
Use `<IconButton>` for icon-only actions (settings, theme toggle, close).

```tsx
import { IconButton, SettingsIcon, XIcon } from "./components/ui";

<IconButton onClick={onSettings} aria-label="Settings">
  <SettingsIcon/>
</IconButton>

<IconButton onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close">
  <XIcon/>
</IconButton>
```

Always include `aria-label` for accessibility. Override icon size with `className="w-5 h-5"` on the icon if needed.

title: Card — rounded surface container
content:
Use `` for grouped content with subtle elevation. ```tsx import { Card } from "./components/ui"; // Standard card with default padding

Summary

Total: $1,234

 // Card with no padding (for ListItems) ``` Use `padding="none"` when nesting `` components — they have their own internal padding.

title: ListItem — iOS-style row component
content: iOS-style list row with optional leading icon, title, subtitle, trailing content, and chevron. ```tsx import { Card, ListItem, UserIcon, CheckIcon } from "./components/ui"; } title="John Doe" subtitle="Last seen 2 min ago" trailing={2m} showChevron onClick={() => navigate("profile")} /> } title="Completed task" subtitle="Done yesterday" /> ``` Always wrap multiple ListItems in ``. Props: `leading`, `title`, `subtitle`, `trailing`, `showChevron`, `onClick`.

title: BottomSheet — modal form container
content:
ALL forms MUST go inside ``, triggered by FAB or button. NEVER render forms inline on the page. ```tsx import { BottomSheet, Input, TextArea, Button } from "./components/ui"; const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); setOpen(false)} title="New Task">
setName(e.target.value)} autoFocus />

title: Input and TextArea — form fields
FAB — floating action button
content:
The FAB is the primary way to trigger actions (adding items, creating new entries).

```tsx
import { FAB, PlusIcon } from "./components/ui";

// Standard FAB
<FAB onClick={() => setOpen(true)} label="New item">
  <PlusIcon/>
</FAB>

// With TabBar — add bottom="tabBar" so FAB doesn't overlap
<FAB onClick={() => setOpen(true)} bottom="tabBar">
  <PlusIcon/>
</FAB>
```

The FAB opens a `<BottomSheet>` — it should NEVER perform an action directly. The flow is: tap FAB → BottomSheet opens → fill form → save → close.

title: SegmentedControl — filter pills
EmptyState — zero-data fallback
ThemeProvider and dark mode toggle
Available SVG icons list
content:
20 icons available from the UI library. Import only what you need.

```tsx
import {
  PlusIcon,          // + symbol — add/create actions
  CheckIcon,         // checkmark — completion, success
  XIcon,             // × symbol — close, cancel, delete
  TrashIcon,         // trash can — delete
  SunIcon,           // sun — light mode
  MoonIcon,          // moon — dark mode
  ChevronRightIcon,  // > — navigation, "go to"
  ChevronLeftIcon,   // < — back navigation
  HomeIcon,          // house — home tab
  SearchIcon,        // magnifying glass — search
  SettingsIcon,      // gear — settings tab
  UserIcon,          // person — profile, account
  HeartIcon,         // heart — favorites, likes
  StarIcon,          // star — ratings, bookmarks
  ClockIcon,         // clock — time, history
  CalendarIcon,      // calendar — dates, events
  BellIcon,          // bell — notifications, reminders
  InboxIcon,         // inbox — empty states, messages
  MenuIcon,          // hamburger — menu (avoid — prefer tabs)
  MoreIcon,          // three dots — overflow menu
} from "./components/ui";
```

Override size: `<PlusIcon className="w-5 h-5" />`.

title: Tailwind v4 — CSS-first configuration
content:
Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first configuration. There is NO `tailwind.config.js` file.

Key differences from v3:
- Configuration is in CSS (`global.css`), not JavaScript
- No `@apply` needed (and it breaks outside `@layer`) — just use classes inline
- The `@theme` directive replaces `theme.extend` in the JS config
- All colors, spacing, and utilities work out of the box

```css
/* global.css — already configured, DO NOT MODIFY */
@import "tailwindcss";
```

Just use Tailwind classes in your JSX. No config files needed.

title: Tailwind v4 — dark mode with class strategy
content:
Dark mode uses the class-based strategy. The `dark` class on `` activates all `dark:` variants. ```tsx // Light and dark backgrounds
// Light and dark text

// Dark borders

// Dark card surfaces
``` Every color class should have a `dark:` counterpart. Common pairings: - `bg-white` / `dark:bg-gray-900` (page background) - `bg-gray-50` / `dark:bg-gray-800` (card surface) - `text-gray-900` / `dark:text-white` (primary text) - `text-gray-500` / `dark:text-gray-400` (secondary text) - `border-gray-200` / `dark:border-gray-700` (borders)

title: Tailwind v4 — opacity modifiers
content:
Use the `/` opacity modifier syntax. The v3 `bg-opacity-*`, `text-opacity-*`, and `border-opacity-*` utilities are REMOVED. ```tsx // ✅ Correct v4 syntax
// 80% opacity background
// 50% opacity overlay

// 70% opacity text

// 50% opacity border // ❌ WRONG — removed in v4

``` The `/` modifier works with any color utility: `bg-`, `text-`, `border-`, `ring-`, `shadow-`, etc.

title: Tailwind v4 — spacing and sizing utilities
content:
Common spacing patterns for mobile apps:

```tsx
// Screen edge padding
<main className="px-5">                    // 20px horizontal padding

// Vertical spacing between elements
<div className="space-y-3">                // 12px gap — cards in list
<div className="space-y-4">                // 16px gap — sections
<div className="space-y-6">                // 24px gap — major sections

// Card internal padding
<div className="p-4">                      // 16px all sides

// Bottom padding to clear FAB/TabBar
<main className="pb-32">                   // 128px bottom clearance

// Flex with gap
<div className="flex items-center gap-3">  // horizontal row with 12px gap
<div className="flex flex-col gap-2">      // vertical stack with 8px gap
```

Never use arbitrary values unless necessary — the design scale is: 1(4px), 2(8px), 3(12px), 4(16px), 5(20px), 6(24px), 8(32px).

title: Tailwind v4 — common color patterns for mobile
content:
Mobile-friendly color patterns that work in both light and dark modes:

```tsx
// Primary action colors
className="bg-indigo-500 text-white"                    // primary button
className="bg-indigo-500/10 text-indigo-600 dark:text-indigo-400"  // subtle tag

// Success / completion
className="text-green-500"                              // completed status
className="bg-green-500/10 text-green-600 dark:text-green-400"    // success badge

// Warning / pending
className="text-amber-500"                              // warning status
className="bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400"   // pending badge

// Danger / delete
className="text-red-500"                                // error/delete
className="bg-red-500/10 text-red-600 dark:text-red-400"          // danger badge

// Neutral surfaces
className="bg-gray-50 dark:bg-gray-800"                 // subtle surface
className="bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-800/50"             // input background
```

title: Tailwind v4 — text and typography
content:
Typography scale for mobile apps: ```tsx // Heading hierarchy

// page title

// section title

// card title // Body text

// body

// caption // Numeric/data text {count} // big number {price} // inline number // Truncation

Long text that might overflow...

Text limited to 2 lines...

``` Use `tabular-nums` for numbers that change (counters, prices) so they don't jitter.

title: Tailwind v4 — flexbox patterns for mobile
content:
Common flexbox patterns used in mobile layouts: ```tsx // Horizontal row — icon + text + trailing

{title}

{subtitle}

{time}
// Center everything (empty states, loading)

No items

// Space between (header with action)

Recent

// Wrap layout (tags, chips)
{tags.map(tag => ...)}
``` Key: `flex-1 min-w-0` on the middle element allows truncation. `shrink-0` on trailing prevents squishing.

title: Tailwind v4 — rounded corners and shadows
content:
Standard border radius and shadow patterns:

```tsx
// Rounded corners
className="rounded-xl"      // 12px — cards, modals
className="rounded-lg"      // 8px — buttons, inputs  
className="rounded-full"    // pill shape — tags, avatars
className="rounded-2xl"     // 16px ��� hero cards

// Shadows
className="shadow-sm"       // subtle lift — cards
className="shadow"          // standard elevation
className="shadow-lg"       // prominent — modals, FAB

// Combined card pattern
<div className="rounded-xl bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 shadow-sm p-4">
```

Prefer `rounded-xl` for cards and `rounded-lg` for interactive elements (buttons, inputs).

title: Tailwind v4 — transitions and animations
Things app — task management pattern
content:
Inspired by Things 3 (Cultured Code). Clean task management with minimal chrome. Key patterns: - **Single-purpose screens**: one list per tab, not nested navigation - **Swipe to complete**: visual feedback on task completion (strikethrough + green check) - **Grouped by date**: "Today", "Upcoming", "Anytime" sections - **Subtle categories**: small colored dot next to task, not full colored cards - **Quick entry**: FAB opens bottom sheet with single input + optional fields ```tsx // Task row pattern (Things-style)
{title}
``` Apply to: to-do apps, task trackers, checklists, goal trackers.

title: Streaks app — habit tracking pattern
content:
Inspired by Streaks (Crunchy Bagel). Circular progress, daily completion, streak counts. Key patterns: - **Circular progress ring**: each habit has a completion circle - **Daily grid view**: 7-day row showing completion status per day - **Streak counter**: prominent number showing consecutive days - **Color coding**: each habit gets a distinct accent color - **Tap to complete**: single tap toggles today's completion ```tsx // Habit card with streak (Streaks-style)

{name}

{streak} day streak

{streak}
``` Apply to: habit trackers, daily routines, goal streaks, fitness logs.

title: Linear app — project/issue tracking pattern
content: Inspired by Linear (Linear Inc). Minimal, fast, keyboard-driven feel even on mobile. Key patterns: - **Status pills**: compact colored badges (Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Done) - **Priority indicators**: colored dots or icons (Urgent, High, Medium, Low) - **Flat list with grouping**: group by status or project, collapsible sections - **Quick actions**: swipe or long-press for status change - **Search-first**: prominent search bar at top ```tsx // Status badge pattern (Linear-style) const STATUS_STYLES = { backlog: "bg-gray-100 text-gray-600 dark:bg-gray-800 dark:text-gray-400", todo: "bg-amber-100 text-amber-700 dark:bg-amber-900/30 dark:text-amber-400", in_progress: "bg-blue-100 text-blue-700 dark:bg-blue-900/30 dark:text-blue-400", done: "bg-green-100 text-green-700 dark:bg-green-900/30 dark:text-green-400", }; {label} ``` Apply to: project managers, issue trackers, kanban boards, work logs.

title: iOS Settings pattern — grouped list with sections
content:
Classic iOS Settings-style grouped list layout. ```tsx // Section with heading and grouped items
{/* Section 1 */}

Account

} title="Profile" showChevron onClick={...} /> } title="Notifications" showChevron onClick={...} />
{/* Section 2 */}

Preferences

} title="Dark Mode" trailing={} /> } title="Advanced" showChevron onClick={...} />
``` Apply to: settings screens, profile pages, preference panels.

title: Dashboard pattern — stats cards + recent list
content:
Common dashboard layout: summary stats at top, recent activity below. ```tsx // Stats row — 2 or 3 cards in a horizontal grid

Total

{total}

This Week

{weekly}
// For 3 stats on one row

{val}

Label

 ...
``` Place stats grid above the main list. Works well with `SegmentedControl` below stats for filtering the list.

title: Search pattern — filter list in real-time
Swipe-to-delete pattern
content:
Simple swipe-to-delete UX without a library — use button-based delete instead. Since we don't have a swipe gesture library, use a visible delete button: ```tsx // Option 1: Trailing delete button in ListItem { e.stopPropagation(); remove(item.id); }} aria-label="Delete" > } /> // Option 2: Delete action in BottomSheet details

{selected?.name}

 ``` Prefer Option 2 (confirmation via BottomSheet) for destructive actions.

title: Timer/countdown pattern
Category/tag selection pattern
Pull-down summary card pattern
content:
A hero card at the top of the screen showing key metrics — common in finance and health apps. ```tsx // Summary hero card

Total Balance

${balance.toLocaleString("en-US", { minimumFractionDigits: 2 })}

Income

+${income}

Expenses

-${expenses}

 ``` Works for: budget trackers, expense apps, portfolio summaries, workout totals.

Zustand store with localStorage persistence
Zustand computed selectors
Zustand store for expense/finance tracking

Zustand store for notes/journal app
Tab navigation state pattern
Recharts — bar chart for weekly/monthly data
content:
Use Recharts for data visualization. Style to match the app theme. ```tsx import { BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, ResponsiveContainer, CartesianGrid } from "recharts"; const data = [ { day: "Mon", value: 12 }, { day: "Tue", value: 8 }, { day: "Wed", value: 15 }, { day: "Thu", value: 6 }, { day: "Fri", value: 20 }, { day: "Sat", value: 14 }, { day: "Sun", value: 10 }, ];

This Week

 ``` Key styling: no ``, no `` (cleaner on mobile), `radius` for rounded bar tops, `#6366f1` (indigo-500) as default fill color.

title: Recharts — line chart for trends
content:
Line chart pattern for showing trends over time. ```tsx import { LineChart, Line, XAxis, ResponsiveContainer, Tooltip } from "recharts"; const data = [ { date: "Apr 1", value: 45 }, { date: "Apr 2", value: 52 }, { date: "Apr 3", value: 48 }, { date: "Apr 4", value: 61 }, { date: "Apr 5", value: 55 }, ];

Progress

 ``` Use `dot={false}` for cleaner lines. `type="monotone"` gives smooth curves. Style tooltip to match dark theme.

title: Recharts — pie/donut chart for categories
content:
Donut chart for showing category breakdown (expenses, time allocation). ```tsx import { PieChart, Pie, Cell, ResponsiveContainer } from "recharts"; const COLORS = ["#6366f1", "#f59e0b", "#10b981", "#ef4444", "#8b5cf6"]; const data = [ { name: "Food", value: 400 }, { name: "Transport", value: 300 }, { name: "Shopping", value: 200 }, { name: "Bills", value: 500 }, ];
{data.map((_, i) => )} {/* Center label */}

${total}

Total

{/* Legend */}
{data.map((d, i) => (
{d.name}
))}
```

title: Weekly completion grid — no chart library
BUG: Cards touching each other — missing space-y
BUG: Content touching screen edges — missing px-5
content:
Content must never touch the phone screen edges. The `<main>` element needs horizontal padding.

```tsx
// ❌ BUG — content touches screen edges
<main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto">
  <Card>...</Card>
</main>

// ✅ FIX — add px-5 for edge padding
<main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-5 pb-32 space-y-4">
  <Card>...</Card>
</main>
```

Always include: `px-5` (edge padding), `pb-32` (FAB/TabBar clearance), `space-y-4` (section spacing).

title: BUG: Form inline instead of BottomSheet
content:
Forms MUST be inside ``, NEVER rendered inline at the bottom of the page. ```tsx // ❌ BUG — form visible at bottom of page

Add Task

// ✅ FIX — form in BottomSheet, opened by FAB
setOpen(true)}> setOpen(false)} title="Add Task">
```

title: BUG: Custom button/input instead of library components
BUG: Blank screen when list is empty — missing EmptyState
BUG: Missing ThemeProvider wrapper
BUG: Tailwind v3 syntax in v4 project
BUG: FAB overlapping TabBar
content:
When using both FAB and TabBar, the FAB must have `bottom="tabBar"` to position correctly.

```tsx
// ❌ BUG — FAB overlaps the TabBar
<FAB onClick={() => setOpen(true)}><PlusIcon/></FAB>
<TabBar tabs={...} value={tab} onChange={setTab} />

// ✅ FIX — FAB clears TabBar
<FAB onClick={() => setOpen(true)} bottom="tabBar"><PlusIcon/></FAB>
<TabBar tabs={...} value={tab} onChange={setTab} />
```

Also ensure the main content has `pb-32` to clear both the FAB and TabBar at the bottom.

title: BUG: Missing dark mode on custom elements
content:
Every custom-styled element MUST include `dark:` variants for colors. ```tsx // ❌ BUG — no dark mode

Title

Subtitle

// ✅ FIX — dark variants on every color

Title

Subtitle

``` Rule: if you write a `bg-`, `text-`, or `border-` class, add the `dark:` counterpart.

{items.map(item => ( setSelected(item)}>

{item.name}

{item.subtitle}

 ))}
// Detail sheet setSelected(null)} title={selected?.name ?? ""} > {selected && (

content:
Two-column grid for compact card displays (recipes, categories, products). ```tsx
{items.map(item => ( setSelected(item)}>
{item.emoji}

{item.name}

{item.detail}

 ))}
``` Good for: recipe cards, product catalogs, category browsers, image galleries. Use `aspect-square` for consistent card heights.

BUG: Manifest not updated with app name
BUG: Missing overflow-y-auto on main content
content:
The main content area MUST scroll when content exceeds the viewport.

```tsx
// ❌ BUG — content overflows without scrolling
<main className="px-5 space-y-4">
  {longList}
</main>

// ✅ FIX — add flex-1 overflow-y-auto
<main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-5 pb-32 space-y-4">
  {longList}
</main>
```

The full pattern for `<main>` inside `<Screen>`:
- `flex-1` — fill remaining vertical space
- `overflow-y-auto` — enable scrolling
- `px-5` — edge padding
- `pb-32` — clear FAB/TabBar
- `space-y-4` — section spacing

title: Meal/recipe card pattern
content:
Card layout for food, recipe, or meal tracking apps. ```tsx
{/* Food image/emoji placeholder */}
{meal.emoji}

{meal.name}

{meal.category}

{meal.calories} kcal {meal.protein}g protein
``` Apply to: meal trackers, recipe collections, restaurant menus, grocery lists.

title: Workout/exercise tracking pattern
content:
Pattern for workout logging with sets, reps, and weight. ```tsx // Exercise card with sets

{exercise.name}

{exercise.sets.length} sets
{exercise.sets.map((set, i) => (
{i + 1} {set.weight} kg {set.reps} reps
))}
``` Apply to: workout trackers, gym logs, exercise planners, fitness apps.

title: Reading/book list pattern
content:
Pattern for book tracking, reading lists, or media collections. ```tsx
{/* Book cover placeholder */}
📚

{book.title}

{book.author}

Page {book.currentPage}/{book.totalPages} {Math.round((book.currentPage / book.totalPages) * 100)}%
``` Apply to: reading trackers, movie watchlists, podcast queues, course progress.

title: Plant/pet care tracker pattern
content:
Pattern for care schedules with last-watered/fed indicators. ```tsx function daysSince(timestamp: number): number { return Math.floor((Date.now() - timestamp) / 86400000); }
{plant.emoji}

{plant.name}

{plant.species}

{daysSince(plant.lastWatered) === 0 ? ( Watered today ✓ ) : (

plant.waterDays ? 'text-red-500' : 'text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300' }`}> {daysSince(plant.lastWatered)}d ago

every {plant.waterDays}d

)}
``` Apply to: plant trackers, pet care, medication reminders, maintenance schedules.

title: Tip calculator pattern
content:
Quick calculator layout for tip/split bill apps. ```tsx

Total with Tip

${(bill * (1 + tipPercent / 100)).toFixed(2)}

Tip: ${(bill * tipPercent / 100).toFixed(2)} • Per person: ${((bill * (1 + tipPercent / 100)) / split).toFixed(2)}

{/* Tip percentage selector */}
{[10, 15, 18, 20].map(pct => ( ))}
```

title: Mood journal / emoji selector pattern
content:
Emoji-based mood selection for journal or wellness apps. ```tsx const MOODS = [ { emoji: "😊", label: "Great", color: "bg-green-100 dark:bg-green-900/30" }, { emoji: "🙂", label: "Good", color: "bg-blue-100 dark:bg-blue-900/30" }, { emoji: "😐", label: "Okay", color: "bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-900/30" }, { emoji: "😔", label: "Low", color: "bg-orange-100 dark:bg-orange-900/30" }, { emoji: "😢", label: "Bad", color: "bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900/30" }, ];
{MOODS.map(mood => ( ))}
```

Gradient patterns for hero cards and buttons. ```tsx // Linear gradient className="bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-500 to-purple-500 text-white" // Diagonal gradient className="bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-500 to-cyan-400" // Subtle gradient surface className="bg-gradient-to-b from-gray-50 to-white dark:from-gray-800 dark:to-gray-900" // Gradient text className="bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-500 to-purple-500 bg-clip-text text-transparent font-bold" // Usage in hero card

BUG: Using responsive breakpoints on mobile-only app
content:
DO NOT use responsive breakpoints. The app is phone-only at max 430px.

```tsx
// ❌ WRONG — breakpoints do nothing on mobile
className="sm:grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4"
className="hidden md:block"
className="text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg"

// ✅ CORRECT — design for one fixed mobile width
className="grid grid-cols-2"  // if you want 2 columns, just use it
className="block"            // always visible
className="text-sm"          // one size fits all
```

The Screen component clamps to max-w-[430px]. There is no tablet or desktop layout.

title: BUG: Using deprecated Tailwind v3 @apply
Full-screen centered content (login, loading, welcome)
content:
Pattern for screens with centered content (onboarding, error pages). ```tsx
🎯

Welcome

Some description text here.

``` Use on main to vertically center. Good for: welcome screens, empty app states, error pages, loading screens.

title: Swipeable tab content pattern
LocalStorage helper for simple key-value
Water intake / daily goal tracker pattern
content:
Pattern for tracking daily intake against a goal (water, steps, calories). ```tsx const goal = 8; // glasses const current = useStore(s => s.todayGlasses); const progress = Math.min(current / goal, 1);
{current}

Water Intake

{current} of {goal} glasses

 ``` Apply to: water tracking, step counting, calorie intake, reading goals.

title: Multi-step form / wizard pattern
Firebase Auth integration with useAuth + LoginScreen
NotificationPermission — push notification opt-in
PaywallScreen — pricing plans with Stripe + Capacitor IAP
Convex schema — multi-tenant table with by_tenant index
Convex tenantQuery and tenantMutation helpers
Convex query with withIndex — single and composite
content:
Read tenant data ONLY via `.withIndex("by_tenant…")`. `.filter()` is rejected because it scans the whole table; composite indexes handle every extra filter condition. Use one composite index per common dashboard slice — e.g., "this month's transactions by category" needs `by_tenant_and_category` with a time-bounded scan, "open tasks only" needs `by_tenant_and_completed`, "pinned notes first" needs `by_tenant_and_pinned`.

```ts
// Single-key: all tasks for this user
const all = await ctx.db
  .query("tasks")
  .withIndex("by_tenant", (q) => q.eq("tenantId", ctx.tenantId))
  .collect();

// Composite: open tasks only (index covers the extra .eq)
const open = await ctx.db
  .query("tasks")
  .withIndex("by_tenant_and_completed", (q) =>
    q.eq("tenantId", ctx.tenantId).eq("completed", false))
  .collect();

// Ordered + bounded read
const recent = await ctx.db
  .query("tasks")
  .withIndex("by_tenant", (q) => q.eq("tenantId", ctx.tenantId))
  .order("desc")
  .take(20);
```

Prefer `.take(n)` or `.paginate(opts)` over `.collect()` for large tables. Every additional `.eq()` narrows via the index — define a composite index for each common filter combo.

title: Convex mutation — create, update, delete with ownership guard
Convex schema — related tables with Id references
Convex pagination — paginate() with usePaginatedQuery
Convex scheduled function — cron jobs and delayed tasks
Convex action — calling external APIs from the backend
useQuery — reactive Convex queries in React
useMutation — Convex mutation with withOptimisticUpdate for instant UI
useCollection — typed CRUD wrapper for Convex queries
useConvexMode — detect sandbox vs published deployment
BUG: Missing tenant filter on Convex query
BUG: Using .filter() instead of .withIndex() for tenantId
content:
`.filter()` feels ergonomic but is a performance and correctness trap on tenant-scoped tables. It runs AFTER the index scan, so the engine touches every row regardless of filter.

```ts
// ❌ BUG — scans the whole "tasks" table, filters in-memory. Scanner REJECTS.
const open = await ctx.db
  .query("tasks")
  .filter((q) =>
    q.and(
      q.eq(q.field("tenantId"), ctx.tenantId),
      q.eq(q.field("completed"), false),
    ))
  .collect();

// ✅ CORRECT — composite index covers both conditions; scan is O(matching rows).
const open = await ctx.db
  .query("tasks")
  .withIndex("by_tenant_and_completed", (q) =>
    q.eq("tenantId", ctx.tenantId).eq("completed", false))
  .collect();
```

Add the composite index to the schema:

```ts
tasks: defineTable({ /* ... */ })
  .index("by_tenant", ["tenantId"])
  .index("by_tenant_and_completed", ["tenantId", "completed"]),  // add this
```

Rule of thumb: one composite index per common filter combo. Extra indexes cost a bit of write amplification but are cheap vs full table scans.

HeroCentered — centered marketing hero
content:
Full-width centered hero with eyebrow → headline → subheadline → CTA pair. Use when the user asks for a landing page, marketing hero, or homepage top section.

```tsx
import { HeroCentered } from "@appio/layout-blocks";

<HeroCentered
  eyebrow="New"
  headline="Build apps by describing them"
  subheadline="Turn plain-English ideas into production PWAs."
  primaryAction={{ label: "Get started", href: "/signup" }}
  secondaryAction={{ label: "Watch demo", onClick: () => setOpen(true) }}
/>
```

Sizes: `compact` (secondary sections), `default`, `full` (80vh takeover). Animates in via `cardReveal` — do NOT wrap in another motion container.

title: HeroSplit — two-column hero with visual slot
content: Copy on one side, visual (image/illustration/screenshot) on the other. Stacks on mobile — visual always lands below copy on small screens so the CTA stays above the fold. ```tsx import { HeroSplit } from "@appio/layout-blocks"; } copySide="left" /> ``` Use for product-focused marketing pages with a visual anchor. Animates via `cardReveal` + `listStagger` on the CTA group.

title: FeatureGrid — responsive feature card grid
Testimonials — quote card grid
FooterMulti — multi-column site footer
DashboardStats — KPI card row with trend deltas
content:
Row of stat cards: label + big value + optional delta with trend direction. Trend colors are universal (green/red/muted) regardless of persona — users expect traffic-light semantics on dashboards.

```tsx
import { DashboardStats } from "@appio/layout-blocks";

<DashboardStats
  heading="This week"
  items={[
    { label: "Revenue", value: "$12,340", delta: "+12%", trend: "up" },
    { label: "Churn", value: "2.1%", delta: "-0.3 pts", trend: "down", invertTrend: true },
  ]}
/>
```

Set `invertTrend: true` when down=good (churn, latency, error rate) — the arrow still points down but the color goes green.

title: SettingsPanel — sectioned form layout
PricingTable — tiered pricing cards

OnboardingStepper — multi-step wizard with progress indicator
MarketplaceGrid — product/listing catalog grid
ProfileCard — user profile surface
FaqAccordion — expandable FAQ list
content:
Expandable Q&A list with smooth height animation. `mode="single"` (default) keeps one open at a time; `mode="multiple"` allows independent toggling.

```tsx
import { FaqAccordion } from "@appio/layout-blocks";

<FaqAccordion
  heading="Questions?"
  mode="single"
  defaultOpenIds={["free-plan"]}
  items={[
    { id: "free-plan", question: "Is there a free plan?", answer: "Yes — 3 apps, unlimited previews." },
    { id: "export", question: "Can I export my code?", answer: "Every published app ships with full source." },
  ]}
/>
```

Answers accept JSX — include links, lists, code snippets in answers freely. Keyboard accessible (Tab + Enter/Space).
